data.reasoningText:
Now I have a good understanding of the OrderService class. Let me write comprehensive MSTest unit tests for it. The class has three methods:

1. `CreateOrder(string customerId, List<OrderItem> items)` - creates an order
2. `GetOrder(string orderId)` - gets an order by ID
3. `GetOrdersByCustomer(string customerId)` - gets orders by customer ID

I should test:
- CreateOrder: valid creation, null customerId, null items, empty items, total calculation, multiple items
- GetOrder: existing order, non-existing order
- GetOrdersByCustomer: with orders, without orders, multiple orders for same customer


I'll use the modern MSTest framework with current conventions and attributes.

toolCalls.name: create
bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-d5d3bbdcccc346d6bb011171f5718526/fixtures/modern-mstest && dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -30", "description": "Build and run MSTest tests", "initial_wait": 60}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-d5d3bbdcccc346d6bb011171f5718526/fixtures/modern-mstest && dotnet test 2>&1 | tail -30", "description": "Run tests with full output", "initial_wait": 60}
